title: ‘False’, ‘baseless’: Piyush Goyal, Sergio Gor reject news report claiming India rejected quick trade agreement with US
content:
• US Ambassador to India Sergio Gor and India's Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal rejected claims that India rejected a quick trade agreement with the US.
• Indian goods currently attract a 10% tariff in the US, with Washington proposing steeper tariffs of up to 12.5% on dozens of nations, including India, over allegations of forced labor.
• US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer visited Delhi in June for meetings to finalize a trade deal, though Reuters claimed no interim agreement was finalized during this visit.
